basics.name: Jasper Van
basics.label: Senior Software Engineer | Platform Engineer | AI Agentic Tools
basics.summary: Product-minded engineer focused on turning complex infrastructure and product ideas into usable developer-facing systems. Strongest at driving work from architecture and implementation to deployment and verification. Values shipped products, engineering quality, and tools that people can actually use.

work.name: Li Auto Inc.
work.summary: Led cloud-native platform engineering for internal developer platforms, including function compute, event-driven architecture, service mesh, and Kubernetes delivery. Built a function platform supporting 10,000+ functions and peak traffic around 300,000 QPS, with GitOps operations across hundreds of Kubernetes clusters.
highlights: Built FaaS and event-driven platform capabilities with Knative and Fission
Developed service mesh and service governance capabilities based on Istio, Envoy, and Go SDKs
Implemented CI/CD and GitOps workflows with Argo CD, Flux, Terraform, Pulumi, and infrastructure-as-code practices
Standardized platform components for Kubernetes cluster operations, observability, and service delivery

work.name: Igetget Co., Ltd
work.summary: Designed internal platform systems for API gateway, microservices, Kubernetes delivery, CI/CD, profiling, async jobs, and ecommerce services. Owned the core data plane of a proprietary API gateway that reached 250,000 QPS in load testing and supported major live events.
highlights: Built API gateway routing, middleware, plugin, reverse proxy, and load-balancing capabilities
Developed Kubernetes operators and application delivery abstractions for microservices
Replaced Jenkins-based delivery flows with a Tekton-based CI/CD platform
Supported the migration from monolithic applications to microservices and containerized delivery

projects.name: AgentKanban
projects.description: A multi-agent collaboration platform for helping AI coding agents plan, execute, review, and ship project iterations.
highlights: Designed an agent-first board where humans set product direction and leader agents decompose goals into coordinated worker-agent execution.
Built the ak CLI and daemon workflow to dispatch worker agents into separate worktrees, track progress, and connect work back to pull requests.
Created a visible project-iteration loop where agents can plan, delegate, implement, review, merge, and move work toward completion.

projects.name: AnyManagedAgents
projects.description: A self-hostable Cloudflare control plane for multi-model managed agents and sandboxed agent sessions.
highlights: Designed OpenAPI-backed control-plane APIs for agents, environments, sessions, providers, events, usage, and audit metadata.
Built the Cloudflare foundation with Workers, D1, Durable Objects, OIDC tenancy, React console, and documented deployment flows.
Defined an execution-plane model using Cloudflare Sandbox for cloud sessions and registered runners for self-hosted sessions.

projects.name: FlareAuth
projects.description: Deployable identity infrastructure for hosted sign-in, account management, administration, and standard OIDC.
highlights: Ran Better Auth inside a Cloudflare Worker serving hosted auth pages, OAuth flows, OIDC discovery, admin APIs, and Management API endpoints.
Used Cloudflare D1, Drizzle, Hono, and React to provide schema-managed storage, HTTP APIs, hosted account center, and admin console.
Built workflows for applications, users, connectors, security policy, organizations, roles, API resources, webhooks, readiness checks, MFA, passkeys, and linked accounts.

projects.name: Kiae
projects.description: An open-source cloud-native application platform built on Kubernetes and Istio for developer-friendly application delivery.
highlights: Built a Heroku/Vercel-style developer platform on Kubernetes with Git-based application delivery and automated image builds.
Integrated Istio routing, entrypoints, access control, and multi-environment deployment workflows for cloud-native applications.
Supported multi-language application deployment, multi-cluster operations, and observability with OpenTelemetry.

projects.name: ZPan
projects.description: An open-source S3-native file hosting platform for image hosting, file sharing, and backup.
highlights: Reached 2k+ GitHub stars and supported S3-compatible storage with direct client-to-S3 uploads that bypass server bandwidth.
Supported Cloudflare Workers and Docker deployments for low-ops or self-hosted file hosting.
Built workflows for PicGo, ShareX, API uploads, share links, and CLI-based directory backup.
Added Pro flows for white-label deployments, signed entitlements, quota packages, and metered traffic.
